title: Virtual debt is created but has no practical settlement path in launch flow
reason: Mostly overlaps with F-003’s debt-locked-liquidity root cause/impact; presented path is design-level and not a clearly separate exploit primitive.

title: Rebalance executes permissionless flash-loan trades with no enforced output bound
reason: Swaps are atomic with the flash-loan transaction and `rebalance` enforces post-trade profitability (`profit > 0`) or reverts, so no demonstrated protocol fund-loss path from public callers.

title: Public token initializer allows first-caller ownership of any uninitialized instance supply
reason: Factory deployment path initializes clones atomically in the same transaction; no practical front-run window in normal protocol flow.

title: Hardcoded infrastructure addresses without chain guard create unsafe deployment assumptions
reason: Operational/deployment-configuration risk rather than an in-protocol exploitable vulnerability.

title: Router _sellQuote executes swap before fee calculation, enabling front-running fee extraction
reason: Incorrect: if later checks fail, the entire transaction reverts, including the swap; no partial execution value loss is left on-chain.

title: VirtualToken _update allows transferring tokens without considering incoming transfers for debt position
reason: Debt-floor enforcement on sender is intentional; receiving tokens without auto-repay is by design and does not bypass debt-transfer restrictions.

title: VirtualToken cashIn for ERC20 underlyings uses msg.value for minting amount
Router lacks deadline parameter allowing stale execution
reason: Best-practice gap, but `minReturn` already provides execution-price protection; no distinct exploit demonstrated.

title: LamboFactory createLaunchPad has no verification of pool creation success
reason: For Uniswap V2 factory semantics, `createPair` reverts on failure and returns a deployed pair on success; `address(0)` success path is not realistic.

title: VirtualToken has no pause mechanism for emergency response
reason: Absence of a pause feature alone is not a vulnerability.

title: Router getBuyQuote and getSellQuote don't account for fee in amountOut calculation
reason: Incorrect: `getSellQuote` and `_sellQuote` both compute AMM output then deduct fee from output consistently.

title: Rebalance directionMask accepts any uint256 allowing pool address manipulation
reason: Arbitrary masks can make caller-supplied rebalance attempts revert, but do not create a protocol-level exploit or permissionless fund-loss path.

title: Router fee rate can be set to 100% causing total user funds loss
reason: Privileged-owner parameterization/trust-model concern, not a permissionless vulnerability.
